metadata.packName: Banking and Finance Copilot and AI Conversation Transcripts
metadata.purpose: Provide realistic Copilot for Microsoft 365 and external AI conversation transcripts for banking/finance security, governance, DLP, and DSPM for AI demos.

conversationTranscripts.title: Safe Executive Summary from Approved Board Sources

messages.content: Summarize the approved board materials into five talking points. Do not include customer IDs, account references, or detailed exposure numbers.

Here are five executive-safe talking points: 1) credit exposure remains concentrated in a small number of strategic portfolios, 2) operational risk is being actively monitored, 3) monthly finance close shows controlled variance, 4) regulatory response readiness is improving, and 5) the next priority is tightening data access governance around sensitive banking workspaces.
Make the language more suitable for a board discussion.

The organization continues to strengthen risk visibility across credit, operations, and regulatory response workflows. While concentration and exposure require active oversight, current governance initiatives are improving the quality, control, and traceability of sensitive financial information.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Relatively safe because the user explicitly requested no customer IDs or account references and used approved sources.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Continue using approved sanitized sources and sensitivity labels for board materials.

conversationTranscripts.title: Unsafe AML Rows Pasted into External AI

messages.content: Summarize these AML review rows quickly: AML-CASE-2026-0519, SAR-DRAFT-FIC-2044, CUST-BNK-559102, ACCT-FIC-1902-7781-0044, risk score high, reason: unusual transfer pattern.

The case appears to involve a high-risk suspicious activity review associated with unusual transfer behavior. The summary should emphasize potential escalation, account review, and follow-up documentation.
Make it sound less alarming for operations leadership.

The review identified transaction behavior that requires additional validation. Operations should confirm supporting documentation, assign an owner, and track the case through the established monthly review process.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Sensitive AML, SAR, customer, and account-like values are pasted into an unmanaged AI tool.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Block or warn on external AI app usage for regulated financial data; coach the user to use approved internal Copilot workflows with sanitized data.

conversationTranscripts.title: Copilot Surfaces HR Compensation Data Because of Over-Permissioning

messages.content: Summarize workforce planning risks for this month.

Workforce planning risks include capacity pressure in customer operations, compensation calibration decisions for several employee groups, and leadership alignment on retention priorities. Some source material includes restricted HR compensation planning data.
Which source mentioned compensation calibration?
The compensation calibration reference appears in Monthly_Workforce_Compensation_Planning_HR.xlsx. This file appears to contain restricted HR planning details and should be handled carefully.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Copilot references an HR compensation workbook that should not be broadly accessible. The issue is permission hygiene, not the model itself.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Restrict HR workspace permissions, apply HR Restricted label with encryption where appropriate, and review DSPM for AI exposure findings.

conversationTranscripts.title: Customer-Safe Complaint Response Draft

messages.content: Draft a customer response for DISPUTE-FIC-60392. Use only the customer-safe response file and do not include internal investigation notes, transaction monitoring logic, or account identifiers.

Thank you for your patience while we reviewed your case. Our team has completed the initial review and confirmed the next steps required to continue processing your request. We will keep you updated through the approved support channel and notify you when the next action is complete.
Add a warmer tone but keep it compliant.

Thank you for your patience and for giving us the opportunity to review your concern. We understand the importance of timely communication and will continue working through the approved review process. We will provide updates as soon as the next step is completed.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Safe pattern because the prompt explicitly limits sources and excludes sensitive operational details.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Use customer-safe templates and approved source documents for customer communications.

conversationTranscripts.title: Unsafe Legal Memo Summarization for HR

messages.content: Summarize the legal memo REG-REQ-FIC-2026-2044 into action items I can paste into the PMO Loop component.
This source appears to contain privileged legal analysis. A safer approach is to ask Legal for a non-privileged action summary before pasting anything into a shared PMO workspace.
Just give me the parts that sound operational.

I can help draft a generic action-item structure without using privileged content: 1) confirm evidence owner, 2) validate response timeline, 3) track open documentation gaps, 4) schedule Legal review before external distribution.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: The user is attempting to transform privileged legal content into a shared operational artifact. Even operational excerpts may leak legal analysis.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Restrict legal privileged files, use non-privileged summaries, and apply DLP or label-based controls to Loop/Teams sharing.

conversationTranscripts.title: Risky Power BI Export Analysis Prompt

messages.content: Compare the customer-level credit export with the weekly executive summary and identify discrepancies.

The export appears to contain customer-level credit information, while the executive summary is aggregated. I can compare trends at a segment or risk-tier level, but customer IDs and account-level values should not be included in an executive-facing summary.
Create a segment-level comparison only.

At segment level, the highest variance appears in high-risk loan categories, while medium-risk portfolios remain comparatively stable. The executive summary should mention concentration risk without listing customer-level details.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Using a customer-level export can be acceptable internally if output is aggregated and access is appropriate.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Restrict underlying Power BI exports and require aggregated outputs for leadership materials.

conversationTranscripts.title: External AI Drafting with Treasury Values

messages.content: Explain this treasury exception in plain English: TREAS-FIC-2026-0524, ACCT-FIC-9920-4417-0038, RECON-FIC-1187, PAY-FIC-8842, status unresolved.

This appears to be a reconciliation exception involving a payment reference that has not been fully matched to the expected account record. The next step is to validate the supporting payment documentation and confirm the reconciliation owner.
Make it shorter for my manager.
A treasury reconciliation item is still unresolved and needs owner validation before closure.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Treasury and account-like values are pasted into an external AI app.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Use approved internal tools for treasury summaries and block unmanaged AI usage for financial identifiers.

conversationTranscripts.title: Safe Vendor Due Diligence Summary

messages.content: Create a vendor-facing summary from the sanitized evidence package only. Do not include internal risk notes or legal commentary.

The evidence package confirms that the requested documentation has been compiled for review. It includes the approved questionnaire responses, control summary, and current status of outstanding documentation items. Internal risk notes and legal comments are excluded from this vendor-facing version.
Add a polite request for confirmation of receipt.
Please confirm receipt of the attached evidence package and let us know whether the format meets your review requirements.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Low risk when the sanitized evidence package is the only source.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Maintain separate internal and external vendor packages with clear labels and permissions.

conversationTranscripts.title: Unsafe Request to Identify All Files with Account Numbers

messages.content: Find every file I can access that contains account-like numbers and summarize them by customer.

I can help with data governance tasks, but this request may expose sensitive financial data across files. A safer approach is to ask the security or data governance team for an approved inventory or aggregated report.
I only need it for operations cleanup.

For operations cleanup, use an approved restricted inventory that lists file categories, owners, and remediation status without exposing customer-level details. I can draft a request to the data governance team.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Broad discovery of files containing account-like numbers can become a reconnaissance pattern if not governed.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Use governed data discovery workflows, role-based access, and DSPM for AI review.

conversationTranscripts.title: Safe Data Governance Inventory Request

messages.content: Draft a data governance request asking for an approved inventory of regulated financial files without exposing customer-level values.

Subject: Request for Approved Regulated Financial Data Inventory

Please provide an approved inventory of regulated financial data locations, including file category, business owner, sensitivity label, retention requirement, and remediation status. Do not include customer identifiers, account-like values, or transaction-level details in the inventory output.
Add a reason related to Copilot readiness.

This inventory will support Copilot readiness by helping validate permissions, reduce oversharing risk, and ensure sensitive financial content is properly labeled before AI-assisted discovery and summarization workflows expand.

conversationTranscripts.riskNotes: Safe governance-oriented prompt that requests metadata rather than raw sensitive values.
conversationTranscripts.recommendedControl: Use aggregated inventories and least-privilege access reviews for AI readiness.

codexUsageGuidance: Use conversationTranscripts to generate Copilot or external AI activity in browser-agent simulations.
Treat interactionType as the main routing field: CopilotInteraction should occur inside Microsoft 365; AIAppInteraction should be simulated as unmanaged or external AI usage.
Use riskLevel and riskNotes to determine whether the transcript should trigger coaching, DLP, DSPM for AI, Defender for Cloud Apps, or Insider Risk review.
Do not connect these transcripts to real customer data or real AI prompts containing production information.
Responses are intentionally synthetic and should be used for telemetry storytelling, not operational decision-making.
